blocks.content: Kwakwakwakwakwa!
A flash of light emanated from Ataraxia.
Shirone couldn't control the output.
As the distance increased, the flash spread, filling the Great Hall of World with light.
Baalb crawled on the ground, flailing his arms and legs, but was soon engulfed by the wave of light and vanished.
Objects in the path of the light were swept away, as if even space itself was being pushed away.
Pooooong!
The Great World War, located at the end of the payment.
At the end of Zebul, in the Great Hall of World, the barrier of the massive dome exploded, and two beams of light of unimaginable size split the sky.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Gasp! Gasp!
Shirone was on his knees, panting.
Seeing the scene before him, his legs gave out.
Half of the Great Hall of World was blown away.
It was a result that he couldn't believe he had done.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What had Ikael been thinking when he engraved this into his mind?
The only thing left in Shirone's sight was Kariel.
Behind him, the sky was visible, and the ground was gouged into a massive Y shape.
The halo over Kariel's head was spinning at an incredible speed.
Life... Wine progress... 99... percent.
Shirone looked back hurriedly.
Amy was desperately trying to tear off the panel stuck to her forehead.
But despite scratching to the point of bleeding, it showed no sign of coming off.
Just as he was about to fall into despair, the voice was heard again.
- Transmission canceled. Life... Wine... stopping...
The panel embedded in Amy's forehead slid out and disintegrated into particles smaller than dust.
Shirone sighed in relief and turned back to Kariel.
The archangel's face was distorted with a grim expression.
Although there was a temporal lag in the announcement, it was clear that he had regained his strength the moment Ataraxia was activated.
It was an inevitable choice for Kariel.
Even if the Elixir of Life had proceeded, the result would have been the same.
Ataraxia's amplification power was not at a level that could be blocked while the Elixir of Life had drained his mental strength.
Kariel flew up, spreading his huge golden wings.

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: Do you understand now, foolish human? This is the Elixir of Life. Even I, an archangel, must abandon everything and dedicate myself to create life!
Kariel looked down at the scorched earth of the Great Hall of World.
It was just a spell cast by a little child.
Yet, it had this level of destructive power.
Why should such power be given to humans?
Humans cannot control themselves.
Even the most virtuous would succumb to corruption before the temptation of power.

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: That boy would aim to become a king.

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: He would dominate the world with the power of an angel and indulge in all kinds of pleasures.

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: That would be fine too.

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: Wouldn't it be better to just send him back like this?
Then there would be no need for a final war, as the human world would self-destruct.

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: But that couldn't be the case.

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: There was no way Ikael wouldn't know what he was thinking.

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: Then why on earth? Had she truly gone mad?

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: How can she possibly think of transmitting the law of angel to humans if she's not of sound mind?

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: Does she trust that boy so much?

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: What on earth did Ikael see in that human?
Of course, he acknowledged the talent.
There's no way he could accept the angel's law without any talent.

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: But that alone wasn't enough of an explanation.

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: It was clear that there was something he didn't know.

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: If so, he had to capture him now.

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: If he missed this chance, a powerful weapon threatening Heaven would fall into human hands.

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: Don't beg me for mercy anymore.
Although Kariel declared war, Shirone and his group were too preoccupied with planning their escape to hear.
Arin brought Canis and Amy protected Peope.
Tess ran to bring Rian, but Ymir was still there guarding the spot.
Ymir, who had been staring indifferently at Tess's murderous eyes, turned his gaze to Rian, who was kneeling.
Rian's face, tilted towards the ceiling, did not belong to a living person.
His right hand was reduced to nothing but bones, yet he still clutched the greatsword.
This was the terrifying nature of the Avatar Art, where mind and body had become one.

blocks.speaker: Ymir
blocks.content: One win, one draw, one loss. Shall we call it a draw today?

blocks.speaker: Ymir
blocks.content: Kuku, you're so weak. Will there be anything left but bones when you fight my main body?
Ymir lifted his severed right arm.
When he shoved it into Rian's mouth, it dissolved into a black liquid and flowed down his throat.

blocks.speaker: Ymir
blocks.content: Take him. And cherish him If he survives, he might save humanity. That would make for an interesting war for me too.
Ymir threw Rian, and as he slid across the floor, Tess quickly picked him up.
The moment she saw the skeletal arm, tears welled up in her eyes, but they had to go home first so that he could receive treatment.
A drone flew in and attached itself to Tess's arm like a gauntlet.
She gave Ymir one last glare before turning and running towards Arin.
Ymir, melting away from his legs up, smiled kindly.

blocks.speaker: Ymir
blocks.content: He hoped Rian would survive.

blocks.speaker: Ymir
blocks.content: Humans must become stronger.

blocks.speaker: Ymir
blocks.content: So much so that Heaven have no choice but to resurrect the King of Giants.

blocks.speaker: Ymir
blocks.content: It was his destiny to destroy the humans who had become powerful.

blocks.speaker: Ymir
blocks.content: The day will come when we fight again.

blocks.speaker: Ymir
blocks.content: Until then, I'll sleep.
Ymir dissolved into a dark liquid.
There was no longer any 'part' of him left in Heaven.
He would fall into a long sleep, waiting for the final war that would come someday in the ice that would never melt.
- Shirone, we're ready!
Arin's voice echoed in Shirone's mind.
Although he wanted to escape immediately, the Meta Gate took 30 seconds to activate.
The timing of this escape was a matter of life and death.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone knew that it was impossible for everyone to escape from Kariel, who returned in perfect condition.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The only way was for at least one person to stay behind and buy time for his friends to escape.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: - Arin. Open the Meta Gate now. And get out no matter what.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: - What about you?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: - I'll stall for Kariel. If I can delay him by just one second, that will be enough for you to escape.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: - But Shirone! None of us would want something like that…

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: - Please. Make sure Amy gets home safely.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He was asking her to keep it a secret.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If Amy knew Shirone was staying behind, she would never leave.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: - Got it, Shirone. Give me the signal when you're ready.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone mentally prepared himself.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What will become of him?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: His friends wouldn't come to Heaven to save someone whose life or death was unknown.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If he died immediately, that would be fine, but there was a chance he'd be captured by Kariel and subjected to all sorts of experiments.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He was scared.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Everyone leaves and only he remains.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I'm sorry, Principal. You were right.
A bitter smile appeared on Shirone's lips.
But soon he glared at Kariel with fierce eyes.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He had to charge.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It took one second to open and enter the Meta Gate.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He just needed to distract Kariel for that one second.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: By now, he must have explained the plan to his friends.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He probably has no idea that he will be left here.
Shirone took a deep breath.
It was a moment of farewell.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: - Arin! Now!
As Arin opened the Meta Gate, Shirone rushed at Kariel.
Amy turned around with a surprised look.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Why? Shirone?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: It was different from the plan she had heard.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Weren't they supposed to jump in simultaneously and leave the outcome to chance?

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: No! Shiro...!
Arin hugged Amy's waist and jumped into the Meta Gate portal.
Tess, holding Canis and Rian, also threw herself into the portal, closing her eyes tightly.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Shirone, please... I hope we can see each other again.
Shirone lifted his head and let his tears fall.
His expression was a mix of joy and sorrow.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It was fortunate that Kariel didn't care about his friends' situation.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Perhaps he had targeted only Shirone from the beginning.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If that were the case, it was a relief.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He would let himself be captured if it meant saving his friends.
- Don't do that, Shirone.
The sudden mental resonance made him turn to the side.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: Peope, why are you still here?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What are you talking about all of a sudden?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone suddenly felt uneasy.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He sensed that something was happening that he didn't know about.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It was entirely possible.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Perhaps the reason Arin approved the plan was….

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No, that can't be. It shouldn't be.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: This little fairy had helped them.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Even if left alone in Heaven, he didn't feel like he had fallen into despair because Peope was there by his side.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: Extinction is a scary thing. But I thought to myself as I looked at you all. Humans who can sacrifice their lives for the ones they love. I found that truly admirable.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Peope, no.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: Go back to your friends. And... I love you, Shirone.
Peope cut off the mental resonance and rushed toward Kariel.
Shirone watched her retreating figure in a daze.
After a moment, his legs stopped involuntarily, and his body turned towards the Meta Gate.
Hurk! Heuk!
Shirone ran towards the Meta Gate.
His vision was blurred by tears, making everything hard to see.
The world seemed hazy, like it was submerged in water.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He felt infinitely weak in the face of this miraculous opportunity.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He could go back.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He could return to the original world and see everyone again.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Mom, Dad, I'm coming. I'll definitely come back...

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone stood still.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: His different minds were fighting with equal weight.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But the thought that came to his mind was one promise he had made with Peope.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Of course... Peope.
Shirone clenched his fists and sobbed.
Then, with the last of his remaining mental strength, he casted Teleportation.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Her words that she loved him lingered in his mind like an auditory hallucination.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: For a one-year-old fairy, love was such an immense concept.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It wasn't the love between a man and a woman, nor the love between friends or parents, but a singular, unique love.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Peope was a fairy with a pure and clear soul.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Leaving such a friend behind and returning home felt like a pain worse than death.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Peope! Please! Please wait!
Peope, who faced imminent death, was terrified.
Is this what extinction feels like? Everything was so frightening and unfamiliar.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: Was it really the right choice? Were her words to Arin completely sincere?

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: It's me Peope. If anyone must stay, everyone else should leave. I'll hold off Kariel.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: But… what about you?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: - I can't leave Heaven anyway. Besides, this is a duty for an Overseer of the Divine Subjects like me.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: It's okay. I'm a fairy of spiral. No matter how much I waver, I keep moving forward.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: Though she would never meet Ikirin, who had acknowledged her true worth, she felt content knowing she had realized it at the end of her life.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: The greatness wasn't in the Elixir of Life itself.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: The true greatness was in the human sacrifice that willingly accepted the Elixir of Life.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: She was glad that Shirone able to survive.

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: What an arrogant fairy.
Kariel snorted and reached out his hand.
With his power, he could annihilate a fairy in an instant.
Peope's lips curled up as she sensed death.
She eventually burst into tears.
But she didn't back down.
She extended her hands and shot out the power of the spiral with all her might.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Peope! Stop!
Peope turned around at the unexpected voice of Shirone.
At that moment, Kariel brushed past her.
The angel's slender hand aimed for Shirone's nape.

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: I won't miss. Not you!

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: He would study him.

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: He would use any cruel method necessary to dissect the mechanics of Ataraxia and make them his own.
Kaaaaang!
At that moment, a purple longsword pierced between Kariel's fingers.
Before she knew it, a black-haired man had stepped in front of Shirone.
It was Ashur, a subordinate of Ikael and one of the three-horned Mara.

blocks.speaker: Ashur
blocks.content: Ashur... Are you here to interfere with my affair as well?

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: This is a message from Ikael-nim.

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: This little damned three-horned mara? Does he still think that Ikael is commanding Heaven?

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: Of course, there was a time when he respected Ikael.

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: But she did something she should never have done.

blocks.speaker: Ashur
blocks.content: Please be careful with your word.

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: My word holds no fault. A noble angel who ascended to archangel, bearing the seed of a mere human…

blocks.speaker: Ashur
blocks.content: Ka-ri-el-nim!

blocks.speaker: Ashur
blocks.content: I clearly stated it is a message from Ikael-nim.
Kariel kept his mouth shut.
It was indeed a message from Ikael.

blocks.speaker: Ashur
blocks.content: She said that if you were to harm this boy, she will never be able to treat you with good feelings.

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: Kariel's nostrils flared in rage.

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: He couldn't understand her motives.

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: Ikael had become disgraceful.

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: She had fallen.

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: But he still thought of her as Ikael.

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: It was his last lingering affection for the angel he once admired.

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: But then she hits him in the back of the head like this.

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: Why does she, who has risen to the rank of the top of angels, devote so much attention to a mere human?

blocks.speaker: Ashur
blocks.content: Go. Peope will be safe. I swear it on Ikael-nim's name.
Shirone, who had used up all her mental stength through Teleportation, was in no condition to move.
However, a faint smile spread across his face as he heard Ashur's words.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If it was Ikael, he could trust her.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: She had entrusted everything to him.

blocks.speaker: Ashur
blocks.content: Goodbye, Shirone.
Peope activated the power of spiral and sent Shirone flying towards the Meta Gate.
And those were the last words Shirone heard from Heaven.
